caption.headline: Born knowing ((nothing at all.))
caption.subline: No rules, no lessons. It just lives here - and the little rays are its senses.

caption.headline: One of these is a ((fake copy.))
caption.subline: Same start, same plan. We changed ONE rule: how well it grips the ground. Can you tell which is the copy?

caption.headline: The copy is on the ((right.))
caption.subline: Wrong grip, so it slides a hair too far - every single step. By eye, you would never know.

caption.headline: The real world has rules. The copy just ((guesses.))
caption.subline: The creature is what really happens. The red ring is the copy's guess - close, but wrong every step.

gauge.label: can an outsider spot the fake?

caption.headline: From outside, the fake is ((easy to catch.))
caption.subline: A watcher who knows the real rules replays every move. It catches the fake almost every time.

gauge.label: does the creature's own mind know?

caption.headline: But the creature itself ((never notices.))
caption.subline: We read its mind each time the fake glitches. Does it catch them? No - a coin flip. It never had a reason to care.

caption.kicker: UNLESS ITS LIFE DEPENDS ON IT
caption.headline: Now make the fake ((cost it dinner.))
caption.subline: Now the copy's bad physics makes food hard to catch. To eat, it must tell real from fake - so it learns. Unasked.

endcard.headline: The fake was detectable all along. The creature only noticed when survival made it matter.
endcard.foot: Real runs, 10 each. 50% = coin flip, 100% = always right. Read out, never rewarded.
